task_name: Write permit stage 3C live sandbox trigger and runbook
goal: Add the Bluepilot-side live 3C trigger for exactly one permit-gated sandbox write. The trigger must be closed by default, require an explicit confirmation phrase, accept only a permitId plus base64 UTF-8 content for one fixed sandbox file, and call smartPush with writePermit so the existing BP-145 byte-output guard performs the corridor consume before any GitHub write.
scope_out: Do not perform a live write during this task.
Do not modify SmartPush, the gate client, the patch adapter, providers, package files, or tools.
Do not add a generic write endpoint or caller-controlled target repo/path.
Do not change maya-core from the Bluepilot repo.
Do not introduce new Opus-branded function, feature, or task names.
claims: A new POST /probe/sandbox-permit-write route is closed unless BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_PERMIT_WRITE_ENABLED=true.
The route requires confirm='permit-write-to-bluepilot-sandbox', a non-empty permitId, and contentBase64.
The route writes only .bluepilot/phase-3c-permit-write.md in G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox on main; target repo, branch, path, op, and baseSha are not caller-controlled.
The route decodes contentBase64 into a UTF-8 string and passes that exact content to smartPush with writePermit { permitId, op:'create', branch:'main', baseSha:'' }.
No token, permit secret, or caller input is echoed beyond permitId, target metadata, result status, and commit summary.
The route calls smartPush directly, not the LLM/provider orchestrator, so the content written is exactly the provided content.
assumptions: maya-core BP-144 stage 3A is deployed and the operator can issue a permit using the paired maya-core script/runbook.
BP-145 is merged on Bluepilot and smartPush already performs hash-at-byte-output plus corridor consume.
Stage 3C live execution is manual after merge/deploy; tests use injected smartPush implementations and perform no GitHub write.
dod: Tests prove the endpoint is 403 when env is off, requires the exact confirmation phrase, rejects caller-controlled fields, decodes contentBase64, and calls smartPush with the fixed sandbox target plus writePermit.
Tests prove method_not_allowed for non-POST and no token-like content is serialized.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
Review packet and live runbook are present.
stop_conditions: The route would need caller-controlled target repo, branch, or path.
The route would need a provider/LLM call to generate the content.
A live write is required to verify the implementation.
SmartPush would need to be modified again.
prior_task_findings: Claude's BP-146 runbook was corrected against local Bluepilot code: /probe/sandbox-real-write still used orchestrateTask and did not pass writePermit to smartPush, so a live permit write needs a narrow direct smartPush trigger. This task adds that trigger without changing the BP-145 byte-output guard.
